token_family_docs.$description: Viewport mode collection — six responsive ranges. Modes: xs, sm, md, lg, xl (the base — no class is applied), 2xl. Each mode carries the CSS class string the application adds to <body> to activate it plus the range bounds (from / to). The collection also holds the breakpoint primitives that the range bounds reference; the upper bound of each range is the next breakpoint minus one pixel so adjacent ranges do not overlap; 2xl is open-ended and has no upper bound. The collection also defines the minimum page width the system supports — the floor for the xs page container, since the xs range itself starts at 0px.

xs.$description: Lower boundary of the xs (extra-small) viewport range.

sm.$description: Lower boundary of the sm (small) viewport range.

md.$description: Lower boundary of the md (medium) viewport range.

lg.$description: Lower boundary of the lg (large) viewport range.

xl.$description: Lower boundary of the xl (extra-large) viewport range.

2xl.$description: Lower boundary of the 2xl (extra-extra-large) viewport range.

min_supported_width.$description: Minimum page width the system supports. The xs viewport range starts at 0px, but layouts are not designed below this width — the xs page container uses this value as its floor.

xs.$description: Developer-facing class. The application adds it to <body> to activate the xs viewport mode, overriding the xl base. Scopes which viewport-dependent token values resolve.

sm.$description: Developer-facing class. The application adds it to <body> to activate the sm viewport mode, overriding the xl base. Scopes which viewport-dependent token values resolve.

md.$description: Developer-facing class. The application adds it to <body> to activate the md viewport mode, overriding the xl base. Scopes which viewport-dependent token values resolve.

lg.$description: Developer-facing class. The application adds it to <body> to activate the lg viewport mode, overriding the xl base. Scopes which viewport-dependent token values resolve.

xl.$description: xl is the base viewport: no class is applied to <body>. It is the first-paint default, rendered before any viewport class is set; other viewports override it via their .ob-viewport-* class. This token is for completeness and consistency; it is not used in code since the absence of a viewport class on <body> indicates the xl viewport mode. Scopes which viewport-dependent token values resolve.

2xl.$description: Developer-facing class. The application adds it to <body> to activate the 2xl viewport mode, overriding the xl base. Scopes which viewport-dependent token values resolve.

from.$description: xs range start, inclusive — the xs breakpoint.

to.$description: xs range end, inclusive — one pixel below the sm breakpoint, so adjacent ranges do not overlap.

from.$description: sm range start, inclusive — the sm breakpoint.

to.$description: sm range end, inclusive — one pixel below the md breakpoint, so adjacent ranges do not overlap.

from.$description: md range start, inclusive — the md breakpoint.

to.$description: md range end, inclusive — one pixel below the lg breakpoint, so adjacent ranges do not overlap.

from.$description: lg range start, inclusive — the lg breakpoint.

to.$description: lg range end, inclusive — one pixel below the xl breakpoint, so adjacent ranges do not overlap.

from.$description: xl range start, inclusive — the xl breakpoint.

to.$description: xl range end, inclusive — one pixel below the 2xl breakpoint, so adjacent ranges do not overlap.

from.$description: 2xl range start, inclusive — the 2xl breakpoint. Open-ended, no upper boundary.
